_comment: Machine-readable index of every example published in docs/EXAMPLES.md. scripts/docs_drift.py re-runs each deterministic:true entry's captured command and diffs expected_anchors against the actual output, so a mutated golden or a drifted excerpt turns CI red. deterministic:false entries are explicitly historical model-run snapshots and are never executed by the offline checker; current live-evidence status is published in docs/BENCHMARKS.md. docs_drift.py also fails if EXAMPLES.md grows a Provenance line no entry covers, so the manifest cannot silently under-cover the doc.

examples.note: Historical model-run snapshot; not replayed offline. The command reruns the current scenario, while docs/BENCHMARKS.md owns the dated current-evidence label.
Historical model-run snapshot; not replayed offline. The command reruns the current scenario, while docs/BENCHMARKS.md owns the dated current-evidence label.

Deterministic golden lint replay. The anchors mechanize EXAMPLES.md's claims: three EARS ACs, one [SECURITY: TZ-001]-tagged AC, one [SURFACE]-tagged AC, a passing lint, and the composed two-task tasks.json. Any structural mutation of evals/golden/EVAL-005 (a dropped tag, a broken trace, a removed task) flips a count or the status and turns this red.

Replays EXAMPLES.md section 4's headline claim: eval_check.py runs all five frozen goldens back through their lints/schema checks and prints '5 golden gate(s)'. Any mutated golden (a broken plan.json, a dropped blocking_high, a missing eligibility clause) fails a gate and turns the eval_check run — and this replay — red. If a sixth golden is added, update section 4's prose and this anchor together.
